location: Guaranteed drop from the Death Knight boss at the bottom of Scorpion River Catacombs, a dungeon in a cave at the western end of the Scorpion River in Rauh Base.
tactics: From the Ravine North grace, head northwest along the Scorpion River to the catacombs entrance; inside, a long hall patrolled by Deathblight Eyes leads to an elevator down to the boss.

There are two separate Death Knight bosses in the DLC — this one in Scorpion River Catacombs drops the Longhaft Axe, while a different Death Knight in Fog Rift Catacombs drops the Twin Axes instead. Make sure you're in the right dungeon for this weapon.

Watch for lightning crackling around his shoulders before his Lightning Dash Grab — a vanish-and-charge grab that can one-shot you and fully heal him if it connects; roll away the instant you see the tell.
He's weak to Holy damage and easily poise-broken — landing two consecutive heavy attacks in a row, or circling in for backstabs, shuts down his offense quickly.
lore: A golden, lightning-charged poleaxe stamped with the image of a decayed ring, carried by the Death Knights who once served as Godwyn the Golden's personal guard. Its electric charge is tied to the old dragon-worshipping cult of the capital, and its bearers were specifically entrusted with watching over the Prince of Death's remains.

builds.name: STR/Faith lightning hybrid
builds.why: D-grade scaling in both Strength and Faith splits its damage between physical and lightning, and its Blinkbolt skill gives a fast, invincibility-framed gap-closer that also serves as burst damage.

builds.name: Faith incantator's backup melee
builds.why: Characters already investing in Faith for incantations get meaningful bonus lightning damage from this axe's FAI scaling without needing much beyond the 16 Strength two-handed requirement.
